Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

bat speed 73.5 mph
swing length 7.4 ft
attack angle 13.9°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.7+13.9*0+9.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-7.2+13.9*0+6.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

2.Patrick BaileyLHBCLE vs Andrew Abbott (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.157 / xBA 0.123 / xSLG 0.168 (-158 pts)K 33.3%BB 4.0%HR 0.6%Hit 13.6%Strong Pitcher

Patrick Bailey LHB vs Andrew Abbott LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 371+1726+1790, pitcher rows 705+2677+2334

Verdict

Abbott's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.157 xwOBA (-158 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +19 pts (bb_type-adj 0.138 -> final 0.157); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Andrew Abbott is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Slider0.10.2100.279GB 37% / LD 22% / FB 29% / PU 12%0.199-110.1630.1240.24430.2

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 70.2 mph
swing length 7.4 ft
attack angle 7.2°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-5.1+2.958-2.2on plane
Sweeper-8.2+15.513+7.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Changeup-6.8+11.234+4.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

3.Zach McKinstryLHBDET vs Trey Yesavage (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.168 / xBA 0.128 / xSLG 0.161 (-147 pts)K 28.3%BB 7.6%HR 1.1%Hit 10.0%Strong Pitcher

Zach McKinstry LHB vs Trey Yesavage RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 296+2180+1278, pitcher rows 243+683+0

Verdict

Yesavage's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach McKinstry should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.168 xwOBA (-147 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.175 -> adj 0.158); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.158 -> final 0.168); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

5.Justin FoscueRHBTEX vs Spencer Arrighetti (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.180 / xBA 0.142 / xSLG 0.250 (-135 pts)K 33.1%BB 1.6%HR 8.2%Hit 8.3%Strong Pitcher

Justin Foscue RHB vs Spencer Arrighetti RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 45+42+187, pitcher rows 472+619+2509

Verdict

Arrighetti's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Justin Foscue should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.180 xwOBA (-135 pts vs league, Pitcher). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

6.Garrett StubbsLHBPHI vs Braxton Ashcraft (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.185 / xBA 0.164 / xSLG 0.196 (-130 pts)K 31.9%BB 9.1%HR 0.4%Hit 10.5%Strong Pitcher

Garrett Stubbs LHB vs Braxton Ashcraft RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 66+4+747, pitcher rows 684+1107+0

Verdict

Ashcraft's curveball is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.185 xwOBA (-130 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.217 -> adj 0.204); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -18 pts (bb_type-adj 0.204 -> final 0.185); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

7.Drew RomoLHBCWS vs Edward Cabrera (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.190 / xBA 0.172 / xSLG 0.357 (-125 pts)K 26.9%BB 15.9%HR 1.5%Hit 7.0%Strong Pitcher

Drew Romo LHB vs Edward Cabrera RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 151+12+189, pitcher rows 725+2253+1734

Verdict

Cabrera's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Drew Romo should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.190 xwOBA (-125 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.302 -> adj 0.309); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -119 pts (bb_type-adj 0.309 -> final 0.190); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drew Romo is on a 14-day slump (-58 pts vs season). Edward Cabrera is in a 14-day rough patch (+63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

8.Ozzie AlbiesLHBATL vs Connelly Early (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.202 / xBA 0.196 / xSLG 0.242 (-113 pts)K 15.9%BB 1.6%HR 1.4%Hit 17.3%Strong Pitcher

Ozzie Albies LHB vs Connelly Early LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 672+2411+1686, pitcher rows 712+401+0

Verdict

Early's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Ozzie Albies should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.202 xwOBA (-113 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -40 pts (raw 0.235 -> adj 0.196); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.196 -> final 0.202); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ozzie Albies is on a 14-day slump (-43 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Verdict

Abbott's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Chase DeLauter should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.218 xwOBA (-97 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.229 -> adj 0.218); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Andrew Abbott is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

10.Colt KeithLHBDET vs Trey Yesavage (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.220 / xBA 0.172 / xSLG 0.235 (-95 pts)K 28.3%BB 7.4%HR 2.0%Hit 15.2%Edge Pitcher

Colt Keith LHB vs Trey Yesavage RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 464+1887+2147, pitcher rows 243+683+0

Verdict

Yesavage's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Colt Keith should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.220 xwOBA (-95 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.231 -> adj 0.210); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.210 -> final 0.220); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Colt Keith is on a 14-day slump (-54 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball47.00.3810.273GB 51% / LD 19% / FB 27% / PU 3%0.354-260.3120.2600.3286.5
Split-Finger39.40.2010.168GB 36% / LD 18% / FB 36% / PU 10%0.190-100.0430.0400.02471.1
Slider13.50.2830.405GB 45% / LD 20% / FB 30% / PU 6%0.250-330.3400.2470.52448.6

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

12.Jorbit VivasLHBWSH vs Shane Baz (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.221 / xBA 0.170 / xSLG 0.232 (-94 pts)K 19.3%BB 5.5%HR 0.9%Hit 14.6%Edge Pitcher

Jorbit Vivas LHB vs Shane Baz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 329+246+0, pitcher rows 774+2724+1207

Verdict

Baz's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Jorbit Vivas should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.221 xwOBA (-94 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.217 -> adj 0.204); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.204 -> final 0.221); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shane Baz is in a 14-day rough patch (+53 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Jakob Marsee LHB vs Ian Seymour LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 777+881+0, pitcher rows 311+999+0

Verdict

Seymour's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jakob Marsee should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.222 xwOBA (-93 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -14 pts (raw 0.255 -> adj 0.241); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -19 pts (bb_type-adj 0.241 -> final 0.222); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Travis Bazzana LHB vs Andrew Abbott LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 242+0+0, pitcher rows 705+2677+2334

Verdict

Abbott's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.228 xwOBA (-87 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.238 -> adj 0.228); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Andrew Abbott is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 67.5 mph
swing length 8.1 ft
attack angle 6.1°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-5.1+1.911-3.1swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Sweeper-8.2+6.1*0-2.1on plane
Curveball-9.1+6.1*1-3.0on plane
Changeup-6.8+6.1*0-0.7on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Verdict

Early's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Harris II should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.228 xwOBA (-87 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.216 -> final 0.228); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Michael Harris II is on a 14-day slump (-107 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

16.Drew MillasLHBWSH vs Shane Baz (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.230 / xBA 0.209 / xSLG 0.282 (-85 pts)K 19.4%BB 6.5%HR 1.0%Hit 16.7%Edge Pitcher

Drew Millas LHB vs Shane Baz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 281+209+252, pitcher rows 774+2724+1207

Verdict

Baz's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Drew Millas should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.230 xwOBA (-85 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -24 pts (raw 0.249 -> adj 0.225); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Shane Baz is in a 14-day rough patch (+53 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

17.Justin CrawfordLHBPHI vs Braxton Ashcraft (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.234 / xBA 0.184 / xSLG 0.313 (-81 pts)K 26.6%BB 10.3%HR 0.8%Hit 14.4%Edge Pitcher

Justin Crawford LHB vs Braxton Ashcraft RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 491+0+0, pitcher rows 684+1107+0

Verdict

Ashcraft's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Justin Crawford should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.234 xwOBA (-81 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.243 -> adj 0.249); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.249 -> final 0.234); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

18.Liam HicksLHBMIA vs Ian Seymour (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.235 / xBA 0.198 / xSLG 0.345 (-80 pts)K 17.8%BB 6.3%HR 0.9%Hit 17.9%Edge Pitcher

Liam Hicks LHB vs Ian Seymour LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 552+1537+0, pitcher rows 311+999+0

Verdict

Seymour's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Liam Hicks should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.235 xwOBA (-80 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +14 pts (raw 0.275 -> adj 0.289); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -54 pts (bb_type-adj 0.289 -> final 0.235); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

19.David HamiltonLHBMIL vs Joe Ryan (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.237 / xBA 0.167 / xSLG 0.312 (-78 pts)K 23.8%BB 5.3%HR 2.1%Hit 18.4%Edge Pitcher

David Hamilton LHB vs Joe Ryan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 435+744+1191, pitcher rows 660+2798+2108

Verdict

Ryan's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while David Hamilton should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.237 xwOBA (-78 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.242 -> adj 0.233); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. David Hamilton is on a 14-day slump (-94 pts vs season). Joe Ryan is in a 14-day rough patch (+41 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

20.Chase MeidrothRHBCWS vs Edward Cabrera (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.240 / xBA 0.236 / xSLG 0.293 (-75 pts)K 22.9%BB 5.9%HR 1.6%Hit 18.1%Edge Pitcher

Chase Meidroth RHB vs Edward Cabrera RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 714+2057+0, pitcher rows 725+2253+1734

Verdict

Cabrera's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Chase Meidroth should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.240 xwOBA (-75 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +17 pts (raw 0.226 -> adj 0.243); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Edward Cabrera is in a 14-day rough patch (+63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Mahle's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.241 xwOBA (-74 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -19 pts (raw 0.257 -> adj 0.239); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Jeff McNeil is on a 14-day slump (-67 pts vs season). Tyler Mahle is in a 14-day hot stretch (-75 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

22.Brady HouseRHBWSH vs Shane Baz (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.245 / xBA 0.189 / xSLG 0.273 (-70 pts)K 27.4%BB 4.2%HR 0.3%Hit 22.5%Edge Pitcher

Brady House RHB vs Shane Baz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 590+969+0, pitcher rows 774+2724+1207

Verdict

Baz's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Brady House should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.245 xwOBA (-70 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.231 -> adj 0.216); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +29 pts (bb_type-adj 0.216 -> final 0.245); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Brady House is on a 14-day heater (+48 pts vs season). Shane Baz is in a 14-day rough patch (+53 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

24.Eric HaaseRHBSF vs Aaron Civale (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.247 / xBA 0.216 / xSLG 0.443 (-68 pts)K 30.9%BB 2.4%HR 4.0%Hit 18.0%Edge Pitcher

Eric Haase RHB vs Aaron Civale RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 74+339+270, pitcher rows 719+1803+2735

Verdict

Civale's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Eric Haase should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.247 xwOBA (-68 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +7 pts (raw 0.282 -> adj 0.289); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -42 pts (bb_type-adj 0.289 -> final 0.247); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

25.Ke'Bryan HayesRHBCIN vs Tanner Bibee (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.247 / xBA 0.204 / xSLG 0.304 (-68 pts)K 18.6%BB 4.4%HR 0.5%Hit 21.9%Edge Pitcher

Ke'Bryan Hayes RHB vs Tanner Bibee RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 367+2180+1474, pitcher rows 713+3062+3109

Verdict

Bibee's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Ke'Bryan Hayes should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.247 xwOBA (-68 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -18 pts (raw 0.258 -> adj 0.239); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.239 -> final 0.247); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Tanner Bibee is in a 14-day hot stretch (-49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 26.Alec BohmRHBPHI vs Braxton Ashcraft (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.249 / xBA 0.231 / xSLG 0.288 (-66 pts)K 15.0%BB 3.7%HR 1.4%Hit 21.5%Edge Pitcher

Alec Bohm RHB vs Braxton Ashcraft RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 552+1867+2170, pitcher rows 684+1107+0

Verdict

Ashcraft's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Alec Bohm should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.249 xwOBA (-66 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -27 pts (raw 0.267 -> adj 0.240); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.240 -> final 0.249); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

27.Hunter FeducciaLHBTB vs Janson Junk (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.255 / xBA 0.262 / xSLG 0.361 (-60 pts)K 19.0%BB 10.1%HR 0.0%Hit 18.4%Edge Pitcher

Hunter Feduccia LHB vs Janson Junk RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 227+477+57, pitcher rows 677+1622+175

Verdict

Junk's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Hunter Feduccia should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.255 xwOBA (-60 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.297 -> adj 0.307); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -52 pts (bb_type-adj 0.307 -> final 0.255); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Janson Junk is in a 14-day rough patch (+41 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

28.Xavier EdwardsLHBMIA vs Ian Seymour (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.258 / xBA 0.212 / xSLG 0.351 (-57 pts)K 22.0%BB 4.4%HR 1.2%Hit 22.5%Edge Pitcher

Xavier Edwards LHB vs Ian Seymour LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 723+2260+1192, pitcher rows 311+999+0

Verdict

Seymour's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Xavier Edwards should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.258 xwOBA (-57 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +26 pts (raw 0.246 -> adj 0.272); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.272 -> final 0.258); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

29.Lawrence ButlerLHBATH vs Tyler Mahle (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.261 / xBA 0.176 / xSLG 0.326 (-54 pts)K 29.0%BB 8.8%HR 2.2%Hit 18.3%Edge Pitcher

Lawrence Butler LHB vs Tyler Mahle RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 564+2521+1816, pitcher rows 716+1381+229

Verdict

Mahle's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Lawrence Butler should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.261 xwOBA (-54 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -14 pts (raw 0.266 -> adj 0.252); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.252 -> final 0.261); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Tyler Mahle is in a 14-day hot stretch (-75 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball49.20.3340.259GB 26% / LD 18% / FB 47% / PU 9%0.313-210.2570.1220.35920.6
Split-Finger35.60.2910.271GB 44% / LD 22% / FB 26% / PU 8%0.274-170.2300.2360.25137.6
Cutter12.20.3570.215GB 38% / LD 22% / FB 32% / PU 8%0.374+160.2740.2060.37017.2
Slider3.00.4220.216GB 38% / LD 22% / FB 32% / PU 8%0.425+20.3260.2340.50128.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 72.3 mph
swing length 7.1 ft
attack angle 5.9°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.3+0.6296-3.7swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Split-Finger-7.1+11.547+4.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-5.8+5.059-0.8on plane
Slider-7.0+10.467+3.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

30.Oswald PerazaRHBLAA vs Will Klein (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.267 / xBA 0.195 / xSLG 0.221 (-48 pts)K 30.1%BB 9.9%HR 2.7%Hit 17.2%Slight Pitcher

Oswald Peraza RHB vs Will Klein RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 436+1005+40, pitcher rows 266+391+146

Verdict

Klein's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Oswald Peraza should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.267 xwOBA (-48 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +23 pts (raw 0.238 -> adj 0.260); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.260 -> final 0.267); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

32.Christian VázquezRHBHOU vs Jack Leiter (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.268 / xBA 0.205 / xSLG 0.341 (-47 pts)K 18.2%BB 6.7%HR 2.4%Hit 19.4%Slight Pitcher

Christian Vázquez RHB vs Jack Leiter RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 336+834+1141, pitcher rows 749+2585+669

Verdict

Leiter's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Christian Vázquez should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.268 xwOBA (-47 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.274 -> final 0.268); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jack Leiter is in a 14-day hot stretch (-63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 34.José AzócarRHBATL vs Connelly Early (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.268 / xBA 0.234 / xSLG 0.330 (-47 pts)K 21.6%BB 8.1%HR 0.0%Hit 23.5%Slight Pitcher

José Azócar RHB vs Connelly Early LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 20+67+287, pitcher rows 712+401+0

Verdict

Early's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while José Azócar should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.268 xwOBA (-47 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -6 pts (raw 0.285 -> adj 0.279); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.279 -> final 0.268); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

35.Jackson ChourioRHBMIL vs Joe Ryan (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.270 / xBA 0.189 / xSLG 0.366 (-45 pts)K 28.8%BB 3.1%HR 3.1%Hit 20.9%Slight Pitcher

Jackson Chourio RHB vs Joe Ryan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 159+2228+2163, pitcher rows 660+2798+2108

Verdict

Ryan's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jackson Chourio should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.270 xwOBA (-45 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.261 -> adj 0.254); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.254 -> final 0.270); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Joe Ryan is in a 14-day rough patch (+41 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

36.Bryce JohnsonRHBSD vs Emerson Hancock (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.270 / xBA 0.260 / xSLG 0.389 (-45 pts)K 19.4%BB 2.2%HR 0.3%Hit 21.2%Slight Pitcher

Bryce Johnson RHB vs Emerson Hancock RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 114+298+263, pitcher rows 741+1498+1015

Verdict

Hancock's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Bryce Johnson should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.270 xwOBA (-45 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.303 -> adj 0.286); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.286 -> final 0.270); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Emerson Hancock is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Verdict

Yesavage's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Matt Vierling should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.270 xwOBA (-45 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.261 -> final 0.270); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 38.Andrew VaughnRHBMIL vs Joe Ryan (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.272 / xBA 0.193 / xSLG 0.373 (-43 pts)K 28.6%BB 3.8%HR 3.2%Hit 20.8%Slight Pitcher

Andrew Vaughn RHB vs Joe Ryan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 89+1834+2338, pitcher rows 660+2798+2108

Verdict

Ryan's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Andrew Vaughn should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.272 xwOBA (-43 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -26 pts (raw 0.288 -> adj 0.262); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.262 -> final 0.272); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Joe Ryan is in a 14-day rough patch (+41 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

39.Tyler StephensonRHBCIN vs Tanner Bibee (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.273 / xBA 0.199 / xSLG 0.332 (-42 pts)K 30.4%BB 6.8%HR 2.4%Hit 20.0%Slight Pitcher

Tyler Stephenson RHB vs Tanner Bibee RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 466+1424+1956, pitcher rows 713+3062+3109

Verdict

Bibee's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Tyler Stephenson should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.273 xwOBA (-42 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -25 pts (raw 0.277 -> adj 0.252); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.252 -> final 0.273); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Tanner Bibee is in a 14-day hot stretch (-49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

41.Matt OlsonLHBATL vs Connelly Early (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.276 / xBA 0.200 / xSLG 0.302 (-39 pts)K 40.6%BB 4.4%HR 3.0%Hit 19.9%Slight Pitcher

Matt Olson LHB vs Connelly Early LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 861+3172+3000, pitcher rows 712+401+0

Verdict

Early's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Matt Olson should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.276 xwOBA (-39 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -29 pts (raw 0.263 -> adj 0.233); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +43 pts (bb_type-adj 0.233 -> final 0.276); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 71.5 mph
swing length 7.4 ft
attack angle 8.4°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sinker-5.6+3.0107-2.6on plane
Sweeper-7.7+15.839+8.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
4-Seam Fastball-4.4+3.4110-0.9on plane
Slider-6.6+12.760+6.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Curveball-9.2+16.325+7.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-8.2+17.820+9.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

44.Jhonny PeredaRHBSEA vs Randy Vásquez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.278 / xBA 0.221 / xSLG 0.345 (-37 pts)K 19.0%BB 6.5%HR 0.0%Hit 21.8%Slight Pitcher

Jhonny Pereda RHB vs Randy Vásquez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 21+333+158, pitcher rows 723+2153+1692

Verdict

Vásquez's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jhonny Pereda should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.278 xwOBA (-37 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.298 -> adj 0.288); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.288 -> final 0.278); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Randy Vásquez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

45.Drew GilbertLHBSF vs Aaron Civale (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.279 / xBA 0.206 / xSLG 0.373 (-36 pts)K 16.5%BB 7.3%HR 0.9%Hit 21.1%Slight Pitcher

Drew Gilbert LHB vs Aaron Civale RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 302+416+0, pitcher rows 719+1803+2735

Verdict

Civale's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Drew Gilbert should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.279 xwOBA (-36 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +38 pts (raw 0.234 -> adj 0.272); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.272 -> final 0.279); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Sinker15.30.2040.301GB 29% / LD 29% / FB 35% / PU 7%0.260+560.2460.2030.2758.3
Split-Finger8.50.2050.259GB 41% / LD 30% / FB 27% / PU 2%0.249+440.1930.1690.38824.8
Sweeper2.40.3620.328GB 32% / LD 27% / FB 33% / PU 8%0.396+340.4090.3100.56620.1
Slider1.20.1590.309GB 32% / LD 27% / FB 33% / PU 8%0.198+400.1930.1580.2078.9

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

46.Spencer JonesLHBNYY vs Clay Holmes (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.280 / xBA 0.196 / xSLG 0.211 (-35 pts)K 28.6%BB 21.8%HR 0.0%Hit 14.7%Slight Pitcher

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 64+0+0, pitcher rows 733+2766+1260

Verdict

Holmes' 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Spencer Jones should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.280 xwOBA (-35 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.292 -> adj 0.280); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

49.Anthony VolpeRHBNYY vs Clay Holmes (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.286 / xBA 0.238 / xSLG 0.337 (-29 pts)K 25.2%BB 5.3%HR 2.4%Hit 21.4%Slight Pitcher

Anthony Volpe RHB vs Clay Holmes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 18+2511+3076, pitcher rows 733+2766+1260

Verdict

Holmes' sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Anthony Volpe should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.286 xwOBA (-29 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.286 -> adj 0.269); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.269 -> final 0.286); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

50.Sung-Mun SongLHBSD vs Emerson Hancock (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.286 / xBA 0.161 / xSLG 0.306 (-29 pts)K 25.3%BB 25.2%HR 0.0%Hit 12.0%Slight Pitcher

Sung-Mun Song LHB vs Emerson Hancock RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 70+0+0, pitcher rows 741+1498+1015

Verdict

Hancock's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Sung-Mun Song should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.286 xwOBA (-29 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +5 pts (raw 0.281 -> adj 0.286); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Emerson Hancock is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

  • Low-whiff pitcher meets a disciplined hitter — neither side wins the swing-and-miss battle, expect strikes in the zone and BIP-heavy ABs.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 68.2 mph
swing length 7.4 ft
attack angle 5.4°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.5+5.08+0.5on plane
Sinker-5.7+5.4*2-0.2on plane
Changeup-7.5+5.4*3-2.0on plane
Cutter-7.1+5.15-2.1on plane
Sweeper-7.9+5.4*1-2.5on plane
Slider-8.2+5.4*1-2.7on plane
Curveball-9.8+5.4*2-4.4swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).
